case_name: Blackshear, Jr., M. D., P. A., Blackshear, M. D. v. Haynes

summary.brief: Blackshear, Jr., M. D., P. A., Blackshear, M. D. v. Haynes. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: William Blackshear, M.D., and his medical practice (collectively Blackshear) seek a new trial after a jury found that Dr. Blackshear committed medical negligence and ordered unnecessary diagnostic tests, causing $175,000 in damages to Betty Haynes.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In early 2010, Ms. Haynes' primary care physician referred her to Dr. Blackshear to treat a bruit (a 'whooshing' sound possibly caused by narrowing or obstruction) in her carotid artery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Over the course of several months, the following tests and procedures were performed on Ms. Haynes: ultrasound of the carotids; computed tomography angiography (CTA) with contrast dye on January 29, 2010, that showed stenosis of the right carotid suggestive of FMD; carotid arteriogram on February 15, which found stenosis of the right carotid artery with an appearance suggesting FMD; and a renal vein renin assay procedure on March 10, which indicated FMD in both kidneys.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On April 13, 2010, Dr. Blackshear and Ms. Haynes discussed the results of the March 10 renal vein renin assay procedure and moving forward with an angioplasty on the right artery with an examination of the left artery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hours after the angioplasty, Dr. Blackshear was called back to the hospital due to Ms. Haynes' complaints of back pain. Dr. Blackshear ordered a blood transfusion and CTA upon learning that Ms. Haynes' blood pressure was low.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Haynes presented expert testimony from vascular surgeon Dr. Timothy Harward, who testified that the February 15 carotid arteriogram was unnecessary because Dr. Blackshear had 'already made his diagnosis' from the earlier CTA, which indicated FMD.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Timothy Harward

evidence_index.text: Dr. Harward also opined that the March 10 renal vein renin assay was unnecessary because tests indicated that Ms. Haynes' kidney function was normal and her blood pressure was being controlled with medication.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Timothy Harward

evidence_index.text: Ms. Haynes also presented expert testimony from nephrologist Dr. Harold Helderman, who provided testimony as to both the alleged medically unnecessary tests and medical negligence counts.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Harold Helderman

evidence_index.text: The trial court abused its discretion in allowing Dr. Helderman, a nephrologist, to testify on the standard of care for a vascular surgeon.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Because the determination of whether the decision to perform a procedure turns on what is 'customary' in a given field, Jackson, 469 F. Supp. 2d at 1082, Dr. Helderman's testimony that he would not have ordered the testing Dr. Blackshear ordered was a direct comment on the 'prevailing professional standard of care' for a health care provider engaged in Dr. Blackshear's specialty.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Referred to Dr. Blackshear for treatment of a bruit in the carotid artery, suspected to be caused by stenosis due to fibromuscular dysplasia (FMD).
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Suspected carotid artery stenosis due to FMD

reveal.clinical_significance: Initial assessment of carotid artery stenosis

reveal.result_summary: Stenosis of the right carotid suggestive of FMD
Stenosis of the right carotid artery with appearance suggesting FMD

requestables.name: Renal Vein Renin Assay
requestables.description: Renin assay to assess renal artery function

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient referred to Dr. Blackshear for bruit in carotid artery

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Series of tests and procedures ordered to diagnose and treat suspected FMD

timeline_phases.name: Angioplasty Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Angioplasty performed on right renal artery

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Outcome
timeline_phases.description: Follow-up care and eventual diagnosis of kidney atrophy

key_events.event: Doppler ultrasound shows right kidney atrophy

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with suspected carotid artery stenosis
decision_points.prompt: Should a carotid arteriogram be ordered?

options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant believed it was necessary to confirm diagnosis
Expert testimony indicated prior imaging was sufficient

expected_action_court.description: Should have relied on CT angiography results
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated unnecessary duplication of tests

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony that the arteriogram was unnecessary
explanation.what_should_have_happened: CT angiography should have been sufficient for diagnosis
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with suspected renal artery FMD
decision_points.prompt: Should a renal vein renin assay be ordered?

options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant believed it would confirm FMD

options.description: Do not order renal vein renin assay
options.clinical_reasoning: Expert testimony indicated it was unnecessary given normal kidney function

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant ordered renal vein renin assay

expected_action_court.description: Should not have ordered the assay
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated it was unnecessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony that the assay was unnecessary
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Should have relied on existing tests and patient condition
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Right kidney atrophied to nearly half the size of the left

key_findings: Breach of duty in ordering unnecessary tests
Causation established - unnecessary tests led to kidney damage

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Tests were not clinically indicated and should not have been performed

factual_timeline.event: Patient referred to Dr. Blackshear for bruit in carotid artery
Doppler ultrasound shows right kidney atrophy

tests_performed.result: Stenosis of the right carotid suggestive of FMD
Stenosis of the right carotid artery with appearance suggesting FMD

treatments.outcome: Stent placed in right main renal artery area

court_findings.finding: Defendant breached duty of care by ordering unnecessary tests
